page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, configured with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25 percent at 2924.48. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 5863.06 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3743.0, both falling into the bottom 25 percent. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block read and write bandwidths are average at 44.87 GB/sec and 28.8 GB/sec respectively, the Passmark memory score is poor, placing in the bottom 10 percent. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation tasks are weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highcpu-4 offers a budget-friendly entry point into dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, trading off memory capacity and multi-core scaling for strong single-threaded execution. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is best suited for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints or high-throughput database operations. Suitable workloads include single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with dedicated CPU allocation. It provides 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, 32 MB of L3 cache, and no bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25 percent for stress-ng single-core tests. However, multi-core performance and memory subsystem throughput are generally weak, with Passmark memory scores falling into the bottom 10 percent. The instance maintains average performance in single-threaded compression and text processing. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and qualitative cost-efficiency as a low-cost dedicated compute option, the c2d-highcpu-4 is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute workloads, basic web serving, and text processing tasks that do not require extensive memory or high-speed local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory capacity.

tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and strong single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25 percent
Dedicated CPU allocation with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for single-threaded processing, light web serving, and text processing
